title: Pungent Springy Thai Asparagus Satay
description: A tangy and adventurous Thai street food dish featuring asparagus, this recipe is free from onions and garlic, making it suitable for those with sensitivities. The asparagus is marinated in a mixture of fish sauce, lime juice, and chili flakes, giving it a bold and pungent flavor.
instructions: Soak 20 bamboo skewers in water for 30 minutes to prevent burning.
Cut the asparagus into 1-inch pieces and set aside.
In a blender, combine fish sauce, lime juice, chili flakes, palm sugar, salt, and black pepper, and blend until smooth.
Add the peanut oil to the blender and blend until well combined.
Pour the marinade into a large bowl and add the asparagus, tossing to coat.
Thread 4-5 pieces of asparagus onto each skewer.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Grill the satay for 3-4 minutes per side, or until tender and slightly charred.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped peanuts and cilantro if desired.
